observations.summary: The prepare-tests step created 4 regression guards for injectGitConfig/commit but missed the shell-injection regression guard for injectGitConfig with malicious name/email, confirming the 3.8 hypothesis that prepare-tests creates incomplete regression guards for command-execution call sites.
observations.evidence: Story file NFR Audit section (_bmad-output/implementation-artifacts/3-10-verify-commits-carry-the-users-own-identity.md:344-348): '[NFR][MEDIUM] No shell-injection regression guard for injectGitConfig with malicious name/email — the production code is correct (quoting is in place), but the absence of a regression guard means a future regression could introduce a command-injection vulnerability.' Journal step_end for prepare-tests: '4 regression guards (no --author, no platform account, both config fields, exitCode guard)' — shell-injection guard not among them. Journal step_end for review-nfrs: 'The 2 MEDIUM findings (shell-injection regression guard, getWorkingTreeStatus exitCode check) are the actionable items.' NFR Findings Summary (line 378): 'No shell-injection regression guard for injectGitConfig | Security (NFR-S1) | MEDIUM | Open — recommend patch' (not applied). Ledger entry 39 (Story 3.8) hypothesized this pattern with nextStep to check prior stories' regression guard tests; ledger entry 45 (Story 3.9) deduced non-recurrence because the scope condition was not met. Story 3.10 met the scope condition (regression guards for injectGitConfig which has multiple executeCommand call sites — name and email).

observations.summary: The review-tests step flagged an empty placeholder test stub (from Story 3.3/3.4) with 'Recommendation: Remove' but did not remove it; the empty test with no assertions remains in the file, inflating the test count without verifying behavior.
observations.evidence: apps/agent-be/test/integration/sandbox-lifecycle.integration.spec.ts:122-124 — empty placeholder test still present: it('terminates agent process via Daytona API when sandbox-agent crashes', () => { // Story 3.3/3.4 scope }); — no assertions, passes trivially. Journal step_end for review-tests: 'Flagged for Removal (Earlier Story, Same Directory) — Empty placeholder test at sandbox-lifecycle.integration.spec.ts:122-124... Recommendation: Remove. An empty test that passes trivially inflates the count without verifying behavior.' The review-tests prompt (updated 3.7, 3.9) covers 'stale transitional markers' (comments/headers claiming tests are skipped/disabled/red-phase when active) and 'skipped story-related tests for un-skipping or removal' but does not cover empty placeholder test stubs (active tests with no assertions), so the agent classified it as a recommendation rather than a fix-directly item.

observations.summary: Prior hypothesized observation (ledger entry 38, Story 3.8) — the pattern did NOT recur in 3.10: prepare-tests added it.skip() scaffolds to existing spec files (DP-3: 'Tests added to existing spec files rather than creating new ones'), no new stub files created, and validate-story-2 passed with 0 critical issues; the hypothesis remains open for stories where prepare-tests creates new stub files for TDD red-phase compilation.
observations.evidence: Journal step_end for prepare-tests: '19 tests, all it.skip()' across 3 existing spec files (conversations.service.spec.ts, sandbox.service.nfr-s1.spec.ts, sandbox-lifecycle.integration.spec.ts) — DP-3 decision: 'Tests added to existing spec files rather than creating new ones — avoids duplicating setup.' Journal step_end for validate-story-2: '17 source files / artifacts cross-checked — all references accurate' and 'Story 3.10 Validation — PASS... 0 critical issues.' Ledger entry 38 (Story 3.8) hypothesized: 'this likely recurs on any story where prepare-tests creates stub files for TDD red-phase compilation'; ledger entry 44 (Story 3.9) deduced non-recurrence because the scope condition was not met. Story 3.10 also did not meet the scope condition (no new stub files created).

observations.summary: Stale test-file headers persist for a 5th story (3.4, 3.7, 3.8, 3.9, 3.10); the 3.7 and 3.9 update_steps to review-tests are working as a guard — the agent fixed 2 stale coverage headers directly in 3.10 — but the root cause (prepare-tests creates headers, implement-story un-skips tests without updating them) persists.
observations.evidence: Journal step_end for review-tests: 'Fix 1: conversations.service.spec.ts header (lines 1-19) — Header listed Stories 3.1/3.2/3.5/3.9 but omitted Story 3.10... Fixed: Added Story 3.10 to story list, added coverage line, updated GREEN PHASE note to Story 3.5/3.9/3.10.' and 'Fix 2: sandbox-lifecycle.integration.spec.ts header (lines 9-18) — Covers: list didn't mention Story 3.10's commit-attribution integration tests... Fixed: Added Story 3.10 coverage line documenting AC-1 and AC-2.' Ledger entries 23 (3.4), 32 (3.7), 36 (3.8), 42 (3.9) record prior occurrences; ledger entries 34 (3.7) and 46 (3.9) record update_steps to review-tests. The fingerprint has broadened from red-phase headers to coverage headers that don't mention the new story, but the class is the same: stale test-file headers that don't reflect current state.

observations.summary: Prior confirmed observation (ledger entries 5, 37, 43 — Stories 2.2, 3.8, 3.9) — the pattern did NOT recur in 3.10: the 3.9 update_step (requiring re-read of project-context.md and verification of each pattern before completing) is working, as implement-story proactively applied all applicable NFR patterns to its changes.
observations.evidence: Journal step_end for implement-story: 'All applicable NFR patterns from project-context.md confirmed applied (exitCode check, shell-quoting, test-seam fakes mimic side effects, regression guards assert absence, setImmediate for fire-and-forget, invocationCallOrder for ordering, buildTestModule() canonical factory, ATDD skip removal, [P0] tags, co-located tests).' The 2 MEDIUM NFR findings from review-nfrs are not implement-story gaps: (1) the shell-injection regression guard is a test gap (prepare-tests' responsibility — see atdd-incomplete-regression-guards observation), and (2) the getWorkingTreeStatus exitCode check is pre-existing code from Story 3.1 that Story 3.10 did not modify (deferred per DP-5). Playbook implement-story prompt updatedAt: 2026-07-06T05:41:22.969Z (updatedByRun: 3.9-a1-20260706T022258) — the 3.9 amendment was active for this run (run started 2026-07-06T05:55:17.670Z).

amendments.prompt: Create tests. Before deferring E2E coverage, verify no browser-level mock pattern can simulate the scenario — only defer if no mock covers the ACs, and record the check in the ATDD checklist. When creating regression guards for code that executes external commands with user-controlled input, apply a uniform guard template to every call site: exercise both credential-isolation invariants (no credentials leak via command arguments or environment variables) and input-injection invariants (malicious input is safely quoted and cannot alter the command's behavior). For any decision that arises, consult @_bmad-output/decision-policy.md before escalating: if a rule covers it, decide and continue. HALT only for decisions no rule covers.
amendments.rationale: The prepare-tests step has created incomplete regression guards in 2 stories (3.8: inconsistent env-arg assertions across call sites; 3.10: missing shell-injection guard for injectGitConfig), forcing the review-nfrs step to catch the gaps; directing the agent to apply a uniform guard template covering both credential-isolation and input-injection invariants across all call sites is a low-risk prompt tuning that reduces rework.
